original_hypothesis.title: Ownership mis-assignment risk: ILOManager constructor sets owner to tx.origin, enabling unintended privileged actions before initialize()
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ILOManager constructor calls transferOwnership(tx.origin). If deployment is performed through a contract, relayer, or multisig that does not have tx.origin equal to the intended owner, ownership may be assigned unexpectedly. Additionally, there is a window before initialize() is called where the tx.origin owner can call onlyOwner functions to set feeTaker/implementation/deadlines maliciously.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a known operational footgun that can lead to real production incidents when deployment pipelines use factories/relayers. It is not an on-chain unprivileged exploit, but it is a plausible operational compromise vector with measurable impact (privileged configuration corruption).
deployment_assumptions: ILOManager is deployed via an intermediate contract or workflow where tx.origin is not the intended long-term owner
There is a delay or separate transaction between deployment and initialize()
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Deploy.s.sol deploys ILOManager and then initializes in the same broadcast script, but does not prove atomicity on all deployments; constructor still uses tx.origin.
preconditions: Deployment uses tx.origin not equal to intended owner (e.g., via factory/relayer), or initialize is not executed atomically after deploy
tx.origin-controlled EOA acts maliciously or mistakenly
onlyOwner setters exist and can change critical configuration

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Ownership is set based on tx.origin rather than msg.sender or initializer-provided owner.
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires deployment pipeline weakness; not exploitable by an arbitrary on-chain user without that role.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Between deployment and initialize; also persistent if initialize never called or called with wrong owner.

operational_questions: Is deployment always atomic (deploy + initialize in one transaction) in production?
Are there any factories/relayers used that would make tx.origin differ from the intended owner?
validation_requirements: Review deployment pipeline and confirm deploy+initialize atomicity.
Simulate deployment via contract and confirm owner becomes tx.origin; attempt pre-initialize onlyOwner calls.

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Deploy ILOManager through a workflow where tx.origin is attacker-controlled (or unintended party).
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Before initialize(), call onlyOwner functions to set malicious feeTaker / pool implementation / deadlines.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Allow initialize() to transfer ownership, leaving corrupted config in place.

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Partial-to-full: attacker can set feeTaker, implementation, refund deadline offset, and per-project refundDeadline overrides prior to handoff.
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Medium-High depending on which parameters are changed (could brick launches or redirect fees).
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium: requires owner to notice and revert changes; may be irreversible for already-created projects/pools.
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Global configuration, future pool deployments, fee collection routing.

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Unexpected owner address immediately after deployment; onlyOwner calls before initialize.
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Config values not matching deployment expectations.

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
